channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
item.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.
item.content:encoded:
Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

(Image credit: Rebecca S. Gratz)
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow

item.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
item.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.
item.content:encoded:
Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Foreign ticket holders from World Cup teams' countries won't have to pay bonds to enter U.S.
item.description: The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alex Murdaugh will get a new murder trial. Here's a timeline of his case
item.description: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.

(Image credit: Gavin McIntyre)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Federal government files criminal charges in deadly Key Bridge collapse
item.description: The federal government has filed charges against the companies involved in the deadly 2024 cargo ship crash at Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has filed charges against the companies involved in the deadly 2024 cargo ship crash at Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Scott Maucione

item.title: Some Minneapolis donors have moved on. The immigrants waiting for help haven't
item.description: During Operation Metro Surge, mutual aid efforts raised millions of dollars. But with most agents gone and increasing fatigue from the community, funds are drying up and people are moving on. Immigrants are not.
item.content:encoded:
During Operation Metro Surge, mutual aid efforts raised millions of dollars. But with most agents gone and increasing fatigue from the community, funds are drying up and people are moving on. Immigrants are not.

(Image credit: Tim Evans for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: An intimate look at one military family's life on pause as the Iran war continues
item.description: Military life has always involved some degree of uncertainty. But for many families, the fear and unknowns that come with the Iran war are new territory.
item.content:encoded:
Military life has always involved some degree of uncertainty. But for many families, the fear and unknowns that come with the Iran war are new territory.

(Image credit: Stella Kalinina for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: How the Trump administration has undermined the fight against public corruption
item.description: At least 15 former elected officials and co-conspirators with corruption offenses have been pardoned by President Trump in the last year, undermining the fight against public corruption.
item.content:encoded:
At least 15 former elected officials and co-conspirators with corruption offenses have been pardoned by President Trump in the last year, undermining the fight against public corruption.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Short flights are popular. Will they last?
item.description: Some flights are less than 100 miles, and they're the backbone of the U.S. air system, linking small towns and big cities. But short routes were in decline even before jet fuel costs spiked.
item.content:encoded:
Some flights are less than 100 miles, and they're the backbone of the U.S. air system, linking small towns and big cities. But short routes were in decline even before jet fuel costs spiked.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary resigns after tumultuous tenure
item.description: After a tumultuous 13 months at the helm of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, Marty Makary has resigned.
item.content:encoded: After a tumultuous 13 months at the helm of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, Marty Makary has resigned.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin
